---
title: "CDADE Architecture Notes"
author: "CDADE Team"
date: "2026-07-05"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Architecture

## System overview

CDADE is organized as a three-stage pipeline:

1. L1 detector pool: unsupervised detectors produce anomaly scores over the input series.
2. L2 reconciliation: leaf-level scores are reconciled to enforce coherence across the hierarchy.
3. L3 dynamic selection: a sliding-window selector estimates competence, diversity, and drift to choose the active subset.

## Pipeline flow

```text
Raw counts -> L1 detectors -> L2 reconciliation -> L3 selection -> blended anomaly score
```

The implementation is config-driven and runs through the DVC stages prepare, inject, baselines, detect, reconcile, select, ensemble, evaluate, stats, and ablation.

## Current implementation notes

- The detector stage writes scores to results/detectors.
- The reconciliation stage writes reconciled forecasts to results/reconciliation.
- The selection stage writes blended scores, competence traces, and drift history to results/selection.
- The ensemble stage aggregates the run summary and writes the final JSON payloads under results/ensemble.
- Evaluation, statistical testing, and ablation outputs are written under results/metrics.json, results/stats, and results/ablation.

## Coverage of the README architecture

This chapter now covers the same L1/L2/L3 framing described in the README, including the hierarchical reconciliation objective and the dynamic-selection rationale.
